REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF FIRST UNITARIAN UNIVERSALIST SOCIETY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER IS GRANTED, IN PART, AND DENIED, IN PART. RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER SHALL ISSUE AS TO ASSETS HELD BY DEFENDANT MONTESSORI SCHOOL OF SAN FRANCISCO INC. DBA MONTESSORI SCHOOL OF CHILDREN ONLY, TO SECURE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM OF $284,136.84 ALLEGEDLY DUE UNDER THE CONTRACT. ASSETS HELD BY NON-PARTIES SUNIL WIJESINGHE AND AMALA WIJESINGHE ARE EXEMPT FROM ATTACHMENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2025 Set For Trial On Nov-10-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
Off Calendar Apr-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Wasserman for First Unitarian Universalist Society of San Francisco, Plaintiff (via Zoom).
Thomas Palmer for Montessori School of San Francisco, Defendant (via Zoom).

The Court addressed Plaintiff's evidentiary objections.

Mr. Palmer argued the motion was premature due to ongoing discovery and factual disputes in both the civil and related unlawful detainer actions. He also raised concerns regarding the scope of the proposed attachment. Plaintiff's counsel stated that Plaintiff stood ready to post the required undertaking and referenced a pending motion for summary judgment in the unlawful detainer matter. The Court found Plaintiff had made a sufficient showing to support issuance of a right to attach order, subject to Plaintiff filing an undertaking in the amount of $10,000. After hearing oral arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FIRST UNITARIAN UNIVERSALIST SOCIETY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part. Right to attach order shall issue as to assets held by defendant Montessori School of San Francisco Inc. dba Montessori School of Children only, to secure plaintiff's claim of $284,136.84 allegedly due under the contract. Assets held by non-parties Sunil Wijesinghe and Amala Wijesinghe are exempt from attachment.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
